# Introducing design systems in v0

by

Tabstack by Mozilla

Tabstack by Mozilla

•

You can now create design systems in @v0 by Vercel.

 Create unique color schemes for your v0 generations
 Adjust the colors for a design system right from Design Mode
 Preview in light & dark mode

Their team also is working on support for advanced design systems features like fonts, custom components, AI-generated design systems, and more.

 Watch the product announcement on X →
 Get started with v0.app →

401 views

### Replies

Best

Vatsal M [...] 401 views

### Replies

Best

Vatsal M

This looks like an upgrade to our prototyping process for new SAAS apps. I can now choose across so many colour options for my apps, YAY!  
  
But, for it to be a full on design system it should also allow us to tweak things other than color values like border styles, typography, spacings, grid system, etc. Currently it gives us ability to test our app UI in various color palettes very quickly which is great but having the other design capabilities would make it a complete 'design system' system.

Report

Tabstack by Mozilla

@vatsmi spot on! it definitely needs to support more design tokens. it's in the roadmap afaik, looking forward to it.

results.content:
Zown

used v0 by Vercel to build Zown

•5 reviews

We use v0 to generate production-ready components from natural language prompts. It is core to our design system and helps us move faster by turning ideas into working prototypes almost instantly.

#### What's great

prototyping (9)production-ready code (5)design system integration (2)

 5 views

PromptCraft

used v0 by Vercel to build PromptCraft

•6 reviews

Because PromptCraft turns your raw ideas into AI prompts you can plug directly into v0 to instantly prototype UIs. It’s one of the first tools I tested PromptCraft’s prompts with, and it works like magic together

 3 views

Vibesuite

used v0 by Vercel to build Vibesuite

•3 reviews [...] easy to use (2)prototyping (9)time to market reduction (3)

Good for the previews and easy push to Github. First project and I'm out of credits after 5 prompts. Their affiliate "invite and earn" does not work. Tried with a friend, neither of us got credits. No live chat on the site to get help with this.

#### What needs improvement

unreliable affiliate program (1)not suitable for full production (4)

Report

 63 views

NightScreen Mode

used v0 by Vercel to build NightScreen Mode

•10 reviews

Used it to quickly generate clean UI ideas and components, saving tons of time during early design experiments. I used v0's figma import feature to import my figma design so that I can see how it'll look like. It's amazing.

results.title: v0 by Vercel Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What needs improvement

It can over-assume structure when the prompt is vague, so you get further by iterating in small steps than asking for everything at once. Tighter control over reusing an existing design system and component library would help on

larger projects, so new output matches what is already there instead of needing manual alignment.

#### vs Alternatives

Lovable
bolt.new

Generic chat models give you code but no real design sense, and you spend the time wiring it into a stack yourself. v0 wins because it is built around React and Tailwind, the components drop straight into a Next.js project, and

the design quality out of the box is higher. Less cleanup, faster to something I would actually ship.

Karimuddin CS

#### What's great [...] For those who live off design, need agility, and want to put ideas into practice at lightning speed, v0 is indispensable. Believe me.

⚠️ WARNING

This product can be addictive and is totally contraindicated for those who still want to continue living the "hard" way of life.

Thank you, v0!

#thisismyvibe

Website Leaderboard
Matthew Dvertola
Dustin Harber

#### What's great

#### What needs improvement

Andrei Rybakov

#### What needs improvement

Instead of speeding things up, it keeps generating awkward, broken results that you have to redo by hand. You end up spending more time fixing its output than if you had built it yourself from scratch. [...] On top of that, it just burns through your budget. It’s easily one of the most useless and overpriced tools I’ve used. You’re paying premium prices to debug its mistakes.

Honestly, it’s much easier to run Claude Code locally and get solid, consistent results. With v0, it feels like you’re funding an unfinished experiment rather than using a real product.

#thisismyvibe

#### What needs improvement

Andrei Rybakov

#### What needs improvement

Instead of speeding things up, it keeps generating awkward, broken results that you have to redo by hand. You end up spending more time fixing its output than if you had built it yourself from scratch.

On top of that, it just burns through your budget. It’s easily one of the most useless and overpriced tools I’ve used. You’re paying premium prices to debug its mistakes.

Honestly, it’s much easier to run Claude Code locally and get solid, consistent results. With v0, it feels like you’re funding an unfinished experiment rather than using a real product.

Dheeraj Pai

#### What's great

#### What needs improvement

Shekhar K. Sharma

#### What's great

Rifat Hossain [...] Naumaan Zahid

#### What's great

v0 turns a rough idea into a working UI in minutes. I describe what I want, get clean React and Tailwind back, and ship it instead of fighting boilerplate. The output is close enough to production that the edits are small, and the iteration loop is fast enough that I stay in flow instead of stalling on layout. It's now my default first pass for new pages.

#### What needs improvement

It can over-assume structure when the prompt is vague, so you get further by iterating in small steps than asking for everything at once. Tighter control over reusing an existing design system and component library would help on

larger projects, so new output matches what is already there instead of needing manual alignment.

#### vs Alternatives

Lovable
bolt.new [...] #### vs Alternatives

Lovable
bolt.new

Generic chat models give you code but no real design sense, and you spend the time wiring it into a stack yourself. v0 wins because it is built around React and Tailwind, the components drop straight into a Next.js project, and

the design quality out of the box is higher. Less cleanup, faster to something I would actually ship.

Karimuddin CS

#### What's great

I loved v0 to generate landing pages, but as a software architect, I like a bit of thoroughness and polished output. Which is fine; for free landing page generation from a single prompt, it beats many so-called industry leaders in this space. Solid product.

#### What needs improvement
